steps.title: Simplify each option
steps.content:
An equation has no solution when it reduces to a contradiction (like $0 = c$ for $c \ne 0$).

A: $3(x-1) = -3(-x+1) = 3x - 3$ → $3x - 3 = 3x - 3$, true for all $x$ (infinite solutions).
B: $3(x-1) = -3(-x-1) = 3x + 3$ → $3x - 3 = 3x + 3$, gives $-3 = 3$, contradiction.
C: $3(x-1) = 4(x-1)$ → $-x + 1 = 0$, $x = 1$.
D: $3(x-1) = 4(-x+1) = -4x + 4$ → $7x = 7$, $x = 1$.

steps.title: Pick the contradiction
steps.content:
Only B yields the contradiction $-3 = 3$, so it has no solution.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
